sourceMovie.title: Super 8
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Super 8 (2011) as a thriller theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The thriller and science fiction blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Cloverfield
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Super 8 clicked for you, Cloverfield (2008) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and science fiction, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Commuter
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Super 8 clicked for you, The Commuter (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and mystery, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Event Horizon
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Super 8 clicked for you, Event Horizon (1997) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and mystery, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Super 8?
faq.answer: Try Cloverfield, The Commuter, Event Horizon—each shares DNA with Super 8 while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Super 8?
faq.answer: Super 8 (2011) is best described as Thriller, Science Fiction, Mystery. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Super 8 worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this thriller lane.
